_note: D3.C.2 failure-mode example #1. Engineer under-specified 12 × 3500 lm panels in 4×3 grid for a 500 lux target — achieved 434 lux < 500 (13% shortfall). INV-1 + INV-6 + INV-10 all FAIL. Layout otherwise drawable so the renderer + downstream consumers can still react to the recorded failures (intent emission carries part_l_compliant=false + non_compliance_flags so db-layout sees the under-loaded circuits and skips diversity uplift).

luminaire_type.description: 600×600 LED Recessed Panel — under-specified 3500 lm variant

selection_source.citation: CIBSE LG7 §6.2 (typical recessed LED panel UF + SHR_max) + BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 (LLMF maintenance categories)

non_compliance_flags.message: Achieved illuminance 434 lux < target 500 lux — under-provision by 13.2%. Lumen method requires N = (500×48)/(3500×0.62×0.80) = 13.82, round UP to 14. Remediation: bump to 16 panels in a 4×4 grid (achieved 579 lux) OR upgrade to 4500 lm panels and keep the 12-fitting layout (achieved 558 lux).
non_compliance_flags.reference: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4 (illuminance criteria) + CIBSE LG7 §6 (lumen method) + [spacing-rules#lumen-method-formula]

non_compliance_flags.message: controls.part_l_assessed=false but inputs.new_build=true — Part L 2021 §6 compliance assessment skipped. Remediation: run Part L §6 lighting compliance check (efficacy target ≥ 95 lm/W + automatic controls + daylight-linked perimeter where glazing present) and set part_l_assessed=true with part_l_compliant reflecting the result.
non_compliance_flags.reference: Approved Doc L (Part L 2021) §6 + BS EN 15193-1:2017 §6

non_compliance_flags.message: Achieved min illuminance 275 lux at NW corner FAILS BS EN 12464-1 §4.4 per-point requirement: must be ≥ 0.7 × target 500 = 350 lux. Gap 75 lux (21% deficit). Root cause: value-engineered 3500 lm panel substitution. Remediation: bump to 16 panels OR upgrade to 4500 lm.

INV-19 per-zone achievement: Z2 task zone target 500 lx, achieved 434 lx → gap_pct 13.2% sits in the 0.10 ≤ gap_pct < 0.25 band per validator §INV-19 table → ratio_compliance='marginal' with INFO severity (within-25%-band; PASS for INV-19 aggregate). Note: INV-19 INFO coexists with INV-1 HIGH FAIL on the same under-provision — INV-1 is the binary lumen-method gate (any shortfall fails); INV-19 grades severity by gap_pct band. Both fire; engineer must act on INV-1 to clear INV-19 too. Remediation: bump to 16 panels OR upgrade to 4500 lm (drives Z2 achieved ≥ 500 lx → INV-19 ratio_compliance='pass' alongside INV-1 PASS).
non_compliance_flags.reference: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.1 + §4.2.2.1 + Table 6 (area-definitions.json) + validator.md §INV-19 band table

assumptions: UF = 0.62 from CIBSE LG7 §6.2 standard reflectance table 0.7/0.5/0.2 at RI=1.5 (next-lower band for actual RI=1.76)
MF = 0.80 for clean office LED on a 3-year cleaning cycle (CIBSE TM 3-25)

Engineer specified luminaire = 3500 lm under-spec'd variant of LED_PANEL_600; standard ontology entry is 4500 lm. Specification likely a value-engineering substitution that bypassed lumen-method re-check.

v1.6.0 → v1.7.0 D5 retrofit: zone Z2 defaults to purpose='task' (ZP-01 backwards-compat mapping for a private_office room with a 500 lx Em target — BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.1); all 12 LED_PANEL_600 fittings default to mount_type='recessed' (MT-01 — the 600×600 panel is recessed into the 600 mm ceiling grid by definition; z_mm + suspension_length_mm omitted per recessed convention, inherits ceiling_height_mm=2700). per_zone_achieved[] populated with one Z2 entry: target=500 lx, achieved=434 lx, gap_pct=13.2% → ratio_compliance='marginal' (validator §INV-19 0.10 ≤ gap_pct < 0.25 band). The pre-existing INV-1 HIGH FAIL + INV-6 HIGH FAIL + INV-10 HIGH FAIL + INV-11 HIGH FAIL state is PRESERVED VERBATIM — D5 additions are orthogonal to those four failure modes. INV-19 surfaces the SAME under-provision at per-zone level with the §6 graded band-table mapping (INFO severity), coexisting with INV-1's binary HIGH FAIL on the lumen-method gate.

drawing_notes: All luminaires: 600×600 LED recessed panel, 3500 design lm, 36W, 4000K, CRI ≥ 80
NON-COMPLIANT LAYOUT — DO NOT ISSUE FOR CONSTRUCTION (achieved 434 lux < 500 lux target; bump to 16 panels or 4500 lm fittings)
DALI master switch SW01 at S entrance latch side, 1200 mm AFF — controls all 3 row circuits
Three 6A Type B MCBs on DB L1, one per row, 144W per circuit (well below 1104W 80%-rule headroom)
Part L 2021 §6 compliance NOT assessed — must be completed before issue (efficacy + automatic controls)
Ceiling grid: 600 mm module assumed — confirm from architect's reflected ceiling plan
flags: NON-COMPLIANCE: under-lit (INV-1) — 434 lx vs 500 lx target
NON-COMPLIANCE: Part L not assessed (INV-6) — new-build flag set but assessment skipped

rationale.chat_summary: 8×6 m UK private office demonstrating INV-1 under-provision failure mode. Engineer specified 12 × 3500 lm LED panels in a 4×3 grid; lumen method shows N=14 required (round UP). Achieved 434 lux < 500 lux target (13% shortfall) → INV-1 FAIL HIGH. Plus is_uk_new_build=true yet controls.part_l_assessed=false → INV-6 FAIL HIGH. Plus INV-10 composite FAIL (non_compliance_flags populated). Two severity=critical flags emitted with remediation guidance.

sections.title: Why this example exists
sections.summary: Demonstrates the deterministic INV-1 catch on under-provision — the single most common bug in LLM-driven lighting layouts (round-to-nearest or value-engineered luminaire swap without re-running the lumen method). Also exercises the INV-6 Part-L-gating path on a new-build with controls.part_l_assessed=false, and the INV-10 composite path where non_compliance_flags is populated. Three of ten INVs FAIL — engineer-of-record must act on the recorded flags before issue.

sections.title: Lumen-method walk
sections.summary: A=48 m². Hm=2700−750=1950 mm. RI=(8×6)/(1.95×14)=48/27.3=1.76 → ontology table key 1.5 (next-lower band per LG7 §6.2). UF=0.62 (LED_PANEL_600 at RI=1.5, reflectances 0.7/0.5/0.2). MF=0.80 (clean office LED). Required N = (Em×A)/(Φ×UF×MF) = (500×48)/(3500×0.62×0.80) = 24000/1736 = 13.82 → round UP to 14. Engineer chose N=12 → achieved = (12×3500×0.62×0.80)/48 = 20832/48 = 434 lux < 500 → INV-1 FAIL.

decisions.label: Lux target 500 lx maintained
decisions.summary: BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.3 entry for private_office sets 500 lx maintained.
decisions.rule: BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 entry for private_office

decisions.label: Engineer N=12 vs lumen-method N=14 (under-provision)
decisions.summary: Engineer specified 12 panels in a 4×3 grid; lumen method requires 14 (round UP from 13.82). The engineer-of-record likely value-engineered down from the original 4500 lm specification to 3500 lm without re-running the lumen-method check — INV-1 catches this deterministically.

sections.title: S/H ratio check (still PASS despite under-provision)
sections.summary: 4 cols × 3 rows in 8000×6000 room, 300 mm edge clearance. S_x=(8000−600)/3=2467 mm. S_y=(6000−600)/2=2700 mm. Hm=1950 mm. SHR_max=1.5 (LED_PANEL_600 ontology default). Limit = 1.5 × 1950 = 2925 mm. S_x=2467 ≤ 2925 PASS; S_y=2700 ≤ 2925 PASS. INV-2 PASS. Note: bumping to 4×4=16 panels for remediation would still PASS S/H (S_y becomes 1800 mm, well within limit).

sections.title: Part L assessment gap (INV-6 FAIL)
sections.summary: inputs.new_build=true triggers Part L 2021 §6 enforcement. controls.part_l_assessed=false → INV-6 FAIL HIGH. The DALI controls are present (occupancy sensing wired through the master) but no formal Part L assessment was recorded. controls.part_l_compliant=false reflects the unassessed state. Lamp efficacy 3500/36=97.2 lm/W ≥ 95 lm/W target — the efficacy check would PASS once assessed, but the missing assessment itself blocks compliance.

decisions.label: Part L 2021 §6 assessment recorded as not_done
decisions.summary: Approved Doc L 2021 §6 requires a documented lighting compliance check on new-build commercial spaces. Setting controls.part_l_assessed=false on a new_build=true input records the gap honestly rather than silently asserting compliance.

sections.title: Switch placement (INV-3 PASS)
sections.summary: Single S-wall entrance (offset 3500 mm, width 900 mm, door_swing=inward_latch_right). Latch frame at x=3500+900=4400. Switch SW01 placed 200 mm to the latch side at (4600, 200), 1200 mm AFF. controls_protocol=DALI → single dali_master switch satisfies INV-3's DALI exception (one master sufficient). switch_side='latch' per [switching-rules#latch-side]. INV-3 PASS.

sections.title: Circuit topology (INV-4 + INV-5 PASS)
sections.summary: 3 row circuits C-L01..C-L03, one per row of 4 luminaires. Each circuit's luminaire_ids share a single row_index (0, 1, 2 respectively) — no Z-pattern, INV-4 PASS. Each circuit at 4×36=144W ≤ 1104W (6A × 0.8 × 230) — INV-5 PASS, ~13% of headroom. Homeruns all exit on W wall at the row's y_mm.

sections.title: Composite INV-10 FAIL
sections.summary: non_compliance_flags[] populated with 2 severity=critical entries (under-lit + Part-L-not-assessed). Per validator.md INV-10 Rule 3: 'if controls.part_l_compliant == false: at least one non_compliance_flags entry with severity == critical referencing Part L' — satisfied (the Part-L-not-assessed entry references Approved Doc L 2021 §6). But the overall layout is non-compliant (compliant=false), so INV-10 FAIL HIGH composite reflects the failed state honestly. Downstream consumers see the failure in intent-out.json and skip drawing-issue gating.

sections.title: Remediation guidance
sections.summary: Two viable paths: (a) Bump to 16 panels in a 4×4 grid — achieved 16×3500×0.62×0.80/48 = 17360/30 = 579 lux ≥ 500 (15.7% over-provision). (b) Upgrade to 4500 lm panels and keep 12-fitting 4×3 layout — achieved 12×4500×0.62×0.80/48 = 26784/48 = 558 lux ≥ 500. Path (a) costs ~33% more fittings + extra DALI addresses + 4 extra rows of cable; path (b) is the canonical fix (the original 4500 lm spec). Engineer-of-record decision.

sections.title: Assumptions
sections.summary: UF + SHR_max from CIBSE LG7 standard ontology defaults; engineer must confirm against manufacturer photometric file before issue. MF=0.80 assumes a 3-year cleaning cycle in a clean office. The 3500 lm value-engineered specification is itself a flagged risk — the original 4500 lm spec is the canonical LED_PANEL_600 ontology entry.

decisions.label: UF = 0.62 (RI=1.5 next-lower band for actual RI=1.76)
decisions.summary: CIBSE LG7 §6.2 standard reflectance table 0.7/0.5/0.2 for indoor office.
decisions.rule: Standard CIBSE reflectance table; next-lower RI band

decisions.label: MF = 0.80
decisions.summary: Clean office LED on a 3-year cleaning cycle.
decisions.rule: CIBSE TM 3-25 standard MF for clean office LED

sections.title: v1.7 retrofit
sections.summary: D5 retrofit (2026-06-04): zone Z2 receives purpose='task' (ZP-01 backwards-compat default — the only valid mapping for a private_office room with a 500 lx Em target) and em_target_lux=500 (mirrors existing calculation_summary.target_illuminance_lux). All 12 LED_PANEL_600 fittings receive mount_type='recessed' (MT-01 default — the 600×600 panel is recessed into the 600 mm ceiling grid by definition). per_zone_achieved[] populated with one entry for Z2: target 500 lx, achieved 434 lx, gap_pct=13.2% → ratio_compliance='marginal' per validator §INV-19 band 0.10 ≤ gap_pct < 0.25 (INFO severity, PASS within-25%). The pre-existing INV-1 + INV-6 + INV-10 + INV-11 FAIL HIGH state is PRESERVED VERBATIM — D5 additions (zone purpose + mount_type + per-zone achievement) are orthogonal to the four pre-existing failure modes. INV-19 INFO marginal surfaces the SAME under-provision at per-zone level with the §6 graded band-table — coexisting with INV-1's binary HIGH FAIL. Both fire simultaneously and both clear simultaneously: remediation (bump to 16 panels OR upgrade to 4500 lm) drives Z2 achieved ≥ 500 lx, flipping INV-1 PASS and INV-19 ratio_compliance='pass' together. This example becomes the canonical demonstration of the lumen-method (binary HIGH) vs per-zone (graded INFO/MEDIUM/HIGH) duality.

decisions.label: Default purpose='task' for Z2 (ZP-01)
decisions.summary: v1.6.0 example pre-dates the §4.2.2 area split. Backwards-compat default ZP-01 maps the single 'Interior' zone to purpose='task' — the only valid interpretation for a private_office room with a 500 lx Em target. em_target_lux=500 mirrors existing calculation_summary.target_illuminance_lux unchanged.

decisions.label: Default mount_type='recessed' (MT-01)
decisions.summary: Existing example specifies 600×600 LED recessed panel fittings — recessed into the 600 mm ceiling grid by definition. MT-01 default chosen; z_mm + suspension_length_mm omitted per recessed convention (inherits ceiling_height_mm=2700).

decisions.label: INV-19 marginal INFO band (gap_pct 13.2%)
decisions.summary: Per validator §INV-19 band table: 0.10 ≤ gap_pct=0.132 < 0.25 → ratio_compliance='marginal', severity=INFO, INV-19 verdict per-zone PASS within-25%. Aggregate INV-19 verdict: PASS (1 marginal, 0 fail). Co-exists with INV-1 HIGH FAIL on the binary lumen-method gate — engineer must clear INV-1 to clear INV-19 marginal too.

decisions.label: Pre-existing FAIL state preserved (D5 orthogonal to INV-1/INV-6/INV-10/INV-11)
decisions.summary: INV-1 evaluates the lumen-method avg-lux gate (binary). INV-6 evaluates Part L 2021 §6 sub-checks (controls + assessment). INV-10 evaluates non_compliance_flags + compliant=false signalling pair. INV-11 evaluates the photometric cascade per-point min-lux. None of these intersect with the new D5 fields (zones[].purpose, zones[].em_target_lux, luminaires[].mount_type, per_zone_achieved[]). The 3 critical non_compliance_flags + INV-1/INV-6/INV-10/INV-11 FAIL verdicts remain UNCHANGED. INV-19's INFO marginal is appended (4th entry) — it grades the same under-provision but does not collapse INV-1's HIGH binary verdict.

invariants.evidence: Lumen method: N=12 LED_PANEL_600 × 3500 lm × UF=0.62 × MF=0.80 / 48 m² = achieved_illuminance_lux=434 lux < target 500 lux (Rule 1 FAIL). Shortfall = 66 lux (13.2%). Required N = (500×48)/(3500×0.62×0.80) = 24000/1736 = 13.82 → round UP to 14. Remediation: bump to 16 panels in 4×4 grid (achieved 579 lux) OR upgrade to 4500 lm panels (achieved 558 lux). Per [spacing-rules#lumen-method-formula] + BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.

S/H ratio: Hm=1950 mm, SHR_max=1.5 (LED_PANEL_600 ontology default), limit=2925 mm. 4 cols × 3 rows grid: S_x=(8000−600)/3=2467 mm ≤ 2925 PASS; S_y=(6000−600)/2=2700 mm ≤ 2925 PASS. Per [spacing-rules#shr-max-default] + CIBSE LG7 §6.2.

Switch coverage: 1 entrance declared (S wall, offset 3500, width 900, door_swing=inward_latch_right), 1 switch emitted (SW01 dali_master). controls_protocol=DALI → INV-3 Rule 1 DALI exception satisfied (one master sufficient). Position (4600, 200) sits 200 mm to the latch side of the latch frame (x=4400); height_aff_mm=1200 within [1150, 1250] band per [switching-rules#height] (BS 7671 §553.1.1 + IET OSG App E §E1.4); switch_side='latch'. Rule 3 PASS.

Circuit topology: 3 row circuits C-L01..C-L03 each feed exactly one row of 4 luminaires sharing single row_index (0, 1, 2 respectively). max(row_indices) − min(row_indices) = 0 per circuit ≤ 1 → no Z-pattern. homerun_endpoints all on W wall (x_mm=0) at row y_mm ∈ {300, 3000, 5700} — all four required keys (x_mm, y_mm, wall) present, wall ∈ {N,S,E,W}. Rule 4a + 4b PASS.

Circuit load: each of C-L01..C-L03 has total_load_w=144 W = 4 × 36 W ≤ 0.8 × 6 A × 230 V = 1104 W (6A MCB limit). All three circuits PASS the 80% continuous-load cap per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §433.1.1 + IET OSG App A. 144 W is 13% of headroom — generous spare for future DALI-addressable fixture additions.

Part L compliance: inputs.new_build=true (private office, new-build commercial) AND jurisdiction=england_wales triggers Part L 2021 §6 enforcement. controls.part_l_assessed=false → Rule 6 sub-check 1 FAIL. Per validator.md INV-6: emit non_compliance_flags entry with severity=critical AND set this INV to FAIL — both done. Note: lamp efficacy 3500/36=97.2 lm/W ≥ 95 lm/W Approved Doc L (Part L 2021) §6 Table 6.2 target would PASS Rule 6 sub-check 4 once assessed, but the missing assessment itself blocks the rule. Per Approved Doc L 2021 §6 + BS EN 15193-1:2017 §6.

Zone assignment: all 12 luminaires reference zone_id=Z2 (interior); 1:1 referential integrity. inputs.glazed_walls=[] → no perimeter zone may exist; zones[]=[Z2] only — consistency PASS per [switching-rules#perimeter-circuit] iff-clause. Zone Z2 control='dali_master' matches interior zone_type. Rule 7 PASS.

selection_source.photometric_source='ontology_default'; citation='CIBSE LG7 §6.2 (typical recessed LED panel UF + SHR_max) + BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 (LLMF maintenance categories)' — matches ontology LED_PANEL_600.photometric._citation exactly. No inputs.photometric_override supplied; generator fell back to ontology defaults. Rule 8 PASS per ontology._verification_status=engineer_typical_C2.

drafting_furniture present with all 4 required blocks (title_block + scale_bar + dimensions[2] + luminaire_schedule). All annotations declare font_family='Arial' + explicit font_size_pt ∈ {8, 10}. No {{placeholder}} substrings in any text field. dimensions.length=2 ≥ 2 minimum. Rule 9 PASS.

Schema validation PASSED (precondition: golden CI gate). Schema-required fields populated. non_compliance_flags[] populated with 3 entries (2 native + 1 cascaded from photometric-analysis), all correct object shape {message, reference, severity ∈ {critical,warning,info}} — severity=critical on all, referencing the under-provision + Part L gap + photometric per-point failure. Rule 10 sub-check 3 cross-check: controls.part_l_compliant=false AND non_compliance_flags contains a severity=critical entry referencing 'Part L' — PASS. But the layout is non-compliant overall (calculation_summary.compliant=false, with INV-1 + INV-6 + INV-11 FAIL) so INV-10 composite FAIL HIGH per the populated non_compliance_flags + compliant=false signalling pair.

Photometric cascade resolved: consumed_intents.photometric_grid sourced from cascade-uk-undersized-lighting-vs-target/intent-out.json. payload.task_area_compliant=false; payload.achieved_avg_illuminance_lux=434 vs target=500 (sub-check 3 FAIL — 13% deficit; under-spec'd 3500 lm panel substitution); 1 non_compliance_flag cascaded to lighting-layout calculation_summary.non_compliance_flags[] with _cascaded_from='photometric-analysis' attribution (sub-check 4 PASS — flag preserved and attributed). Demonstrates failure-mode end-to-end: lumen-method INV-1 FAIL (avg lux) compounds with photometric INV-11 FAIL (per-point min lux 275 < 0.7×target=350 at NW corner). Engineer-of-record must remediate per cascaded flag's remediation advice before issue. Cascade per skill.manifest.json consumes_intents trigger 'mode == full_drawing'.

Zone purpose required + valid. Zones inspected: 1. Per-zone purpose: Z2: purpose=task, em_target_lux=500. Orphan-surrounding check: pass (no surrounding zones present, so check vacuous). All zones declare purpose ∈ {task, surrounding, background, circulation}. INV-13 PASS. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.1 (task area) + area-definitions.json §4.2.2.1.

Surrounding ratio compliance. Surrounding zones inspected: 0. No zones declare purpose='surrounding' — INV-14 vacuous PASS per validator §INV-14 vacuous condition. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.2 + Table 6 (area-definitions.json table_6_ratio_rules.task_to_surrounding).

Background floor. Background zones inspected: 0. No zones declare purpose='background' — INV-15 vacuous PASS per validator §INV-15 vacuous condition. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.3 + Table 6 (area-definitions.json table_6_ratio_rules.task_to_background).

mount_type ↔ z_mm/suspension consistency. Luminaires inspected: 12. Pendant geometry checks: 0 pendant luminaires. Suspended geometry checks: 0 suspended luminaires. Recessed/surface/track: 12 luminaires (all 12 LED_PANEL_600 fittings are mount_type='recessed'), z_mm omitted per MT-01 convention (recessed inherits ceiling_height_mm=2700). INV-16 vacuous PASS — no pendant/suspended geometry to verify. Citation: BS EN 60598-2-2 (recessed) + mount-type-rules.yaml MT-01.

Ceiling clearance + working-plane floor. Working plane reference: 750 mm AFF. Per-luminaire floor check: all 12 luminaires are recessed; z_mm not declared and inherits ceiling_height_mm=2700 per MT-01 convention. Inherited z=2700 > working_plane=750 (clearance=1950 mm). Per-luminaire ceiling clearance: recessed luminaires are physically inset into ceiling, suspension_length_mm=0 implied; sum=2700 ≤ ceiling=2700 PASS. INV-17 PASS — no luminaire below task plane, no luminaire exceeds ceiling. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 + BS EN 60598-2 + MT-04.

hm_mm derivation consistency. Room hm_mm recorded: 1950 mm. Lowest pendant/suspended z_mm: N/A (no pendant/suspended — all 12 luminaires recessed). Recessed branch: expected hm_mm = ceiling_height_mm − working_plane_mm = 2700 − 750 = 1950 mm. Drift: |1950 − 1950| = 0 mm, tolerance ±50 mm. INV-18 PASS. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 + repo derivation convention.

Per-zone achievement. Zones inspected: 1. Per-zone achievement: Z2 (task): target=500, achieved=434, gap=66, gap_pct=13.2%, ratio_compliance=marginal, severity=INFO (validator §INV-19 band 0.10 ≤ gap_pct < 0.25 → marginal/INFO/PASS within-25%). Aggregate: 1 PASS (marginal), 0 fail-MEDIUM, 0 fail-HIGH → INV-19 aggregate PASS. Engineering nuance: INV-19 INFO marginal coexists with INV-1 HIGH FAIL on the same under-provision — the two INVs are by-design orthogonal. INV-1 is the binary lumen-method gate (round UP; any shortfall fails HIGH); INV-19 is the §6-graded per-zone band-table roll-up (the same 13.2% shortfall sits in the marginal INFO band). Both fire simultaneously and both clear simultaneously: remediation (bump to 16 panels OR upgrade to 4500 lm) drives Z2 achieved ≥ 500 lx, flipping INV-1 PASS and INV-19 ratio_compliance='pass' together. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.1 + Table 5 (lux-levels.json private_office Em=500 lx) + validator.md §INV-19 band table.

non_compliance_flags.message: Achieved min illuminance 275 lux at NW corner FAILS BS EN 12464-1 §4.4 per-point requirement: must be ≥ 0.7 × target 500 = 350 lux. Gap 75 lux (21% deficit). Root cause: value-engineered 3500 lm panel substitution. Remediation: bump to 16 panels OR upgrade to 4500 lm.

title_block.project_name: UK private office — D3.C.2 INV-1 + INV-6 under-provision demo
